reasoning: There is overwhelming, high-quality evidence indicating that a coordinated diplomatic effort explicitly aimed at recognizing a Palestinian state will take place in mid-June 2025. French President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister François Bayrou have both publicly stated that France plans to announce formal recognition of a Palestinian state during an international conference co-chaired with Saudi Arabia, scheduled for June 17-20, 2025. Furthermore, reliable reports confirm that both the UK and Canada have joined this initiative and intend to make a joint announcement. Given that none of these three countries currently recognizes Palestine, the announcement of recognition by any one of them would trigger a YES resolution. The timeline is highly specific and explicitly targets June 2025. While there is a minor risk that diplomatic pressure, geopolitical events, or a semantic shift (e.g., announcing a "roadmap to recognition" instead of immediate statehood) could delay or dilute the event, the sheer momentum behind this G7-led initiative makes failure or long delay unlikely. Therefore, the probability is assessed at 88%.
